title: Trading Vault Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

excerpt: Complete breakdown of Trading Vault pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
Trading Vault Pricing Overview

Trading Vault is a structured trading journal built for traders who want to combine performance tracking with psychology-focused discipline. The pricing model is refreshingly simple: a free tier for serious traders just starting out, and a Pro plan at $14.99/month for those who want advanced analytics and deeper insights.

Unlike many trading tools that gate core journaling behind a paywall, Trading Vault lets you build a complete trading journal habit on the free plan—no expiration date, no sudden "upgrade-or-lose-your-data" tricks. This makes it one of the most accessible entry points into structured journaling, especially for forex and MT5 traders.

All Trading Vault Plans Compared

Feature Free Forever Pro
Price $0/month $14.99/month
Plan → Execute → Review Workflow ✓ ✓
Basic Trade Journaling ✓ ✓
MT5 Broker Sync (Auto Import) ✓ ✓
Basic Performance Analytics ✓ ✓
Psychology Tools & Discipline Tracking ✓ ✓
Advanced Analytics & Detailed Reports — ✓
Priority Support — ✓
Multi-Market Support (Stocks, Futures, Crypto) Forex focused Expanded
Data Export & Reporting Limited ✓

Annual Pricing: Trading Vault does not publicly advertise an annual billing discount. Both tiers are billed monthly, with no prepay option mentioned. If you commit monthly at $14.99/mo, the annualized cost would be approximately $179.88 per year for the Pro plan.

Free Plan / Free Trial

Trading Vault's free tier is genuinely worth your time—it's not a limited trial masquerading as a free plan. You get:

  • Unlimited trade journaling with no expiration or data limits
  • MT5 broker sync to auto-populate your trades (major time-saver for forex traders)
  • The core Plan → Execute → Review workflow that encourages consistent discipline
  • Basic performance analytics to track win rate, average win/loss, and equity curve
  • Psychology-focused tools for emotional discipline tracking
  • Intuitive, clean interface praised by users for reducing friction

The free plan is designed for traders who want to build a journaling habit without financial commitment. Many profitable traders use only the free tier indefinitely. You lose access to advanced reporting, data export, and priority support, but the journaling core is intact.

When to upgrade from Free to Pro: Move to Pro when you've established a consistent journaling routine (typically 4–8 weeks) and want deeper pattern analysis, custom reports, or tax/accountability exports. If you're still in "trial phase" with journaling, stay free.

Hidden Costs and Fees

Trading Vault's pricing is transparent, but there are a few indirect costs traders should know about:

  • No direct platform fees—Trading Vault doesn't charge exchange fees or data feed costs. Your broker covers those.
  • MT5 broker requirement—If you trade forex but don't use MT5, you'll need to manually log trades or switch platforms (not Trading Vault's cost, but a hidden friction point).
  • No built-in tax software—You may need a separate tool (TurboTax, etc.) to reconcile journal exports for tax season.
  • No third-party integrations—Trading Vault doesn't sync with other platforms (TradingView, cTrader, etc.), so multi-platform traders will face manual data entry.
  • No mobile app cost, but mobile-first traders lose convenience—The web-only design means on-the-go journaling is slower on phones. This isn't a fee, but a usability cost.

For a typical forex trader on MT5, there are no surprise costs. For stock or crypto traders, or those using multiple platforms, expect additional friction or tool costs to fill gaps.

Trading Vault Pricing vs Competitors

How does Trading Vault stack up against other trading journals in the market?

Tool Free Tier Pro Price Best For
Trading Vault ✓ (Unlimited) $14.99/mo Forex traders, psychology-focused, MT5 users
Edgewonk (approximate) Limited trial ~$30–$50/mo Day traders, options, advanced analytics
TradingView Journal ✓ (Basic) Included in Pro (~$15/mo) All markets, charting + journaling
Finny (approximate) ✓ (Limited) ~$9.99–$19.99/mo Beginners, mobile-first, stocks/crypto

Trading Vault's price advantage: At $14.99/month, Trading Vault is one of the cheapest Pro offerings for serious traders. Edgewonk costs 2–3× more. TradingView Journal is comparable but bundled with charting (which may be overkill for pure journaling). Finny is slightly cheaper but lacks the depth of psychology tools and MT5 integration.

Trading Vault's value advantage: The unlimited free tier is a huge win. Most competitors gate journaling behind a paywall or limit free trades to 10–50 per month. Trading Vault lets you journal indefinitely for free, which is powerful for habit formation.

Where Trading Vault loses: Edgewonk has deeper statistical reporting (monte carlo sims, win rate clusters). TradingView Journal integrates charts. Finny has better mobile UX. None of this matters unless you need those specific features.

Is Trading Vault Worth the Price?

Short answer: Yes—especially for its target audience.

For the Free Plan: Trading Vault free is an absolute no-brainer. If you trade forex on MT5, the automatic broker sync alone saves hours of manual logging. The structured workflow (Plan, Execute, Review) combats emotional trading. Even if you never upgrade, you've got a world-class journaling tool for $0. This is worth trying immediately.

For the Pro Plan at $14.99/month: The upgrade makes sense if you've been journaling consistently (4+ weeks) and want:

  • Advanced performance reports to spot patterns (What setups actually work? Which times of day?)
  • Data exports for tax prep or accountability
  • Multi-market support if you've diversified beyond forex
  • Priority support if something breaks during a live trade session

Pro costs roughly $180/year. For a trader making $1,000+ per month from trading, this is trivial ROI. For a beginner struggling to be profitable, skip Pro and master the free tier first. The psychological discipline from structured journaling alone will improve your P&L more than any paid feature.

Who should NOT upgrade:

  • Casual traders who journal sporadically—the free tier is sufficient
  • Traders on non-MT5 platforms (cTrader, Interactive Brokers, futures-only) who'd need manual entry anyway
  • Options traders looking for deep Greeks analysis (Edgewonk is better)
  • Mobile traders who want native app journaling (Trading Vault is web-only)

How to Save on Trading Vault

Honest transparency: Trading Vault does not publicly advertise promo codes, student discounts, or annual prepay savings. The pricing is flat at $14.99/month for Pro.

Ways to effectively reduce your cost:

  • Start free indefinitely. Most traders should use the free tier for 2–3 months minimum. If you're not journaling consistently, no paid plan will help.
  • Share a family account. If another trader you know subscribes to Pro, check if Trading Vault allows account sharing (not explicitly documented, so verify first).
  • Evaluate during trial weeks. If Trading Vault ever offers a Pro trial period, test it before committing to annual renewal.
  • Pair with free tools. Use TradingView Free for charting, Trading Vault Free for journaling, and Excel for tax prep. No subscription bloat required.
  • Bundle with your broker. Some MT5 brokers offer affiliate discounts on third-party tools. Check with your broker's partner program.

At $14.99/month, Trading Vault is already one of the most affordable professional trading journals available. If you're looking for bigger discounts, you're likely overpaying elsewhere in your trading tech stack.

Bottom line: Trading Vault's pricing philosophy is trader-friendly. The free tier is genuinely useful, and the Pro tier is affordable enough to test without financial risk. For forex traders and MT5 users focused on psychology and discipline, this is one of the best value propositions in the trading journal space. Start free, journal consistently for 4–8 weeks, then decide if the Pro features are worth $15/month. Most serious traders will find they are.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
